rows.concept: In-kernel model (oracle-exact forward pass + kernel-owned KV)
rows.what_you_get: A pure-Go model whose KV cache is a kernel-owned Go structure, every rung proven bit-for-bit against a HuggingFace oracle — so the kernel can evict a poisoned span at the KV level.
rows.witness: go test ./internal/model (oracle argmax-exact, per-layer cos=1.0, final-logits max|d|~4.4e-5)
gaps: Reference-grade correctness on a 135M model, not serving-grade throughput on a 7B+ model
The fine-tuned syscall/adjudication model is unbuilt; the fused model is stock SmolLM2
rows.durability_note: The HF oracle witnesses every layer numerically; bit-exactness is a non-regressing cost of correctness enforced by CI.

rows.concept: KV-quarantine bridge (quarantine evicts the K/V span)
rows.what_you_get: When a tool result is quarantined, the kernel mechanically evicts that result's K/V span, leaving the attention cache bit-identical to never having seen the poison.
rows.witness: go test ./internal/kvmmu (max|d|=0 vs never-saw, non-vacuous poison control max|d|~0.33)
gaps: The eviction witness uses a synthetic model (HF numerics proven separately by the oracle)
Not yet wired into the live agent HTTP loop
rows.durability_note: Three-way witness: byte-gate verdict, bit-exact reposition invariant, and eviction confirmed against a non-vacuous control.

rows.concept: Parity lane (parallel matmul + batched prefill, bit-identical)
rows.what_you_get: A parallelized matmul and batched-prefill GEMM whose every output is bit-identical to the serial reference, so the model gets faster with no proven-correctness rung disturbed.
gaps: Small-model parity only; 7B+ f32 throughput unproven
GPU decode parity is a separate backend witness; the int8/Q8_0 SIMD lane is in-flight, not yet [SHIPPED]
rows.durability_note: Bit-identical CPU witness (Float32bits equality) prevents silent drift; CI green on the parity tests.

rows.what_you_get: When the context planner elides a span, the kernel-owned KV residency shrinks to the planner's O(1) resident view byte-for-byte — an O(1) view becomes an O(1) KV footprint.
rows.witness: go test ./internal/kvmmu -run TestApplyPlan (post-plan next-token dist max|d|=0 vs resident-only reference)
gaps: Synthetic model witness; not wired into the live agent HTTP loop
Only SHRINKS residency to the view; paging a span back IN is the separate demand-fault path
rows.durability_note: The eviction is the proven model.KVCache.Evict (re-RoPE+renumber); the plan's faithfulness witness guarantees every elided span carries a page-back-in handle.

rows.concept: RadixAttention parity (prefix-tree KV reuse + policy eviction)
rows.what_you_get: A radix-tree KV cache over the kernel-owned cache that reaches a 77-88% hit rate (inside SGLang's published band) with bit-identical reuse-through-an-edge-split and policy-driven eviction.
rows.witness: TestReuseThroughSplitMatchesRecompute (max|d|=0); measured 77.2-88.2% hit rate vs SGLang's 50-99% band
gaps: Wired into the in-kernel serve path (fak serve --gguf, no --base-url) on by default since 68b67c6; stays an optional leaf for the proxy governed-gateway mainline, where fak fronts an upstream that owns the KV (no local model.KVCache to index) — the proxy prefix lever is the byte-faithful cache_control passthrough (shipped)
Device-backend decode (backend != nil) bypasses the prefix tree; reuse is CPU-session-only today
Measured on synthetic few-shot / multi-turn / tree-of-thought shapes; live production telemetry deferred
rows.durability_note: SGLang numbers confirmed verbatim vs the NeurIPS 2024 proceedings; the eviction path is deterministic (no stochastic learner).

rows.what_you_get: A frozen ABI seam that lets the kernel Evict/Clone an EXTERNAL engine's KV (vLLM/SGLang) zero-copy, so the per-agent quarantine holds against an engine fak does not itself run.
gaps: The arena is an in-process Go []byte STAND-IN; the engine-specific CUDA-IPC / shared-memory transport is the remaining STUB
Requires per-engine integration work behind the frozen ABI before it is usable
rows.durability_note: The SEAM (ABI boundary, zero-copy Resolve, Evict/Clone primitives, opt-in backend swap) is shipped and tested; what remains plugs in with no further ABI change.

rows.concept: Persistent per-session context planner (O(1) resident view)
rows.what_you_get: Replay the heaviest real sessions through the planner and watch resident tokens stay ~13x below linear with every back-reference served as a recoverable page fault — measured, not modeled.
rows.witness: go test ./internal/ctxplan ./cmd/ctxplanbench; -heaviest 5 replays real transcripts (715 turns, 100% of misses served)
gaps: Off the live agent loop (a bench cmd); the agent seam keeps its own heuristic forecast
The forecast is a cheap recency/recurrence heuristic, not a model; a miss costs one page fault, never a lost fact
rows.durability_note: Resident units are a bytes/4 proxy; exact counts reproduce cross-platform; planned recall kept exact on 715/715 turns where compaction destroyed facts on 695/715.

rows.concept: GPU backends (AMD Vulkan / NVIDIA CUDA / Apple Metal)
rows.what_you_get: The in-kernel forward pass runs on real GPUs argmax-exact (cosine 1.0 vs cpu-ref); the NVIDIA CUDA-graph path even hits decode-speed parity with llama.cpp Q8_0 on a model that fits.
gaps: Needs a GPU + a build tag; off by default (the shipped pure-Go binary has zero GPU dependency)
AMD Vulkan f32 is correct but ~58x slower than llama.cpp CPU; token-per-watt stays SIMULATED (no power meter on the box)
rows.durability_note: Numerical parity is witnessed per backend (argmax-exact, cosine 1.0); throughput is claimed only where measured (the NVIDIA decode parity).
